messages: Also, I prefer programs with clear micro-goals each week rather than vague 'get fitter' instructions.
When people talk about progressions, what are practical ways to increase intensity without increasing impact?
If I miss a session, what's a reasonable way to adapt the week's plan so I don't fall off track?
Finally, I like when plans include a short warm-up and cooldown; can that be baked into the sessions rather than treated as optional?

Alright — I'm ready to commit. Could you design an eight-week progressive plan that fits the kinds of schedule constraints and physical limits we've been discussing, and that moves me toward the modest body-composition and endurance improvements I hinted at?
Structure it as a weekly template I can repeat, with clear micro-goals each week so I can see incremental wins.
Please give short early-day session templates that fit those tight pre-work windows, plus a couple of optional longer sessions someone could use when time allows.
For each short session include a warm-up, a main block (strength or low-impact cardio), and a cooldown — all practical and time-efficient.
Give two concrete morning-session examples I can try this week that use only the modest home gear we talked about.
Also include lower-impact swaps and a reduced-intensity option for days when that joint sensitivity flares up, so I can still do something useful.
For progression, outline simple ways to increase challenge (sets, reps, resistance, or interval duration) while keeping impact low.
Recommend quick, easy-to-record metrics I can log each session that show progress without taking much time, and tell me when to pull back.
A couple of practical nutrition tips that fit a busy schedule and support steady, modest fat loss would be helpful too — nothing extreme.
Format the weekly template so it works as a phone-friendly checklist I can tick off each day.
And suggest a plan-B approach for occasions when I can only manage fewer short sessions in a week so I don't feel like the whole plan is ruined.
If you can produce the full eight-week template plus the two example sessions and the quick tracking checklist, that'll be exactly what I need to get started.
